$comment: C23 <stdbit.h> (N3096 §7.18) — FC17.9(b) (D-FULLC-STDBIT). The type-generic bit utilities. This is the ONLY user-facing surface: the `stdc_*` names appear only on `#include <stdbit.h>` (function-like macros spliced by the preprocessor), while the underlying `__builtin_stdc_<op>_<T>` intrinsics are always-injected compiler builtins (c-subset.lang.json builtinFunctions, reserved `__builtin_` namespace) that lower via a width-correct branchless HIR→MIR composition of the 3 hardware bit-count primitives (Popcount/Clz/Ctz) — NO linkable symbol, so NO `library`/`symbols`, available on every object format. For each of the 14 operations: 4 CONCRETE width macros `stdc_<op>_uc/_us/_ui/_ull(x)` route to the matching-width builtin; `stdc_<op>_ul(x)` is a per-FORMAT `variants` macro carrying the LP64/LLP64 `unsigned long` divergence (pe/LLP64 → the 32-bit `_ui`; elf+macho/LP64 → the 64-bit `_ull`); the GENERIC `stdc_<op>(x)` is a 4-WAY `_Generic` over {unsigned char, unsigned short, unsigned int, unsigned long long} — `unsigned long` is DROPPED (audit C1: DSS interns it to a width-core TypeId that COLLIDES with `_ui` on LLP64 / `_ull` on LP64 → S_GenericSelectionAmbiguous; the same width-collapse makes the 4-way route an `unsigned long` operand CORRECTLY on both data models). Each `_Generic` association carries the FULL builtin CALL (never a bare callee) so only the selected arm lowers (the firstNameToken Area-5 mitigation). Plus the 4 object macros: `__STDC_VERSION_STDBIT_H__` and the endianness triple (all DSS targets are little-endian → NATIVE==LITTLE; a big-endian target is D-FULLC-STDBIT-BIG-ENDIAN-NATIVE). The concrete `stdc_*_T` are macros, so `&stdc_leading_zeros_ui` is D-FULLC-STDBIT-ADDRESSABLE-FN (deferred).

macros.name: stdc_leading_zeros
macros.replacement: _Generic((x), unsigned char: __builtin_stdc_leading_zeros_uc(x), unsigned short: __builtin_stdc_leading_zeros_us(x), unsigned int: __builtin_stdc_leading_zeros_ui(x), unsigned long long: __builtin_stdc_leading_zeros_ull(x))

macros.name: stdc_leading_ones
macros.replacement: _Generic((x), unsigned char: __builtin_stdc_leading_ones_uc(x), unsigned short: __builtin_stdc_leading_ones_us(x), unsigned int: __builtin_stdc_leading_ones_ui(x), unsigned long long: __builtin_stdc_leading_ones_ull(x))
